pr.title: itarwinglaser: fix raw pointer arithmetic, add itArwingLaser_ItemVars struct
pr.author: IsaacGemal

summary: Replaced raw pointer arithmetic in `it_802E7A4C` with project-standard item access and a typed `xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser` union member. The key structural fix was adding `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` with explicit leading padding from item offset `0xDD4` to `0xDF4`; without that padding, `xE38` would be laid out at the wrong effective item offset and cause a hidden byte mismatch.

key_files.role: Added `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` with explicit offset-based padding and named fields through `xE44`.
Changed `it_802E7A4C` from raw casts and offsets to `GET_ITEM(gobj)` and `item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38`.

what_changed: Added `itArwingLaser_ItemVars` to `itCharItems.h`, including `_pad0[0xDF4 - 0xDD4]`, `_pad1[0xE18 - 0xDF8]`, and fields documented by item offsets.
Added `itArwingLaser_ItemVars arwinglaser;` to the `xDD4_itemVar` union in `types.h`.
smart_moves: Used `GET_ITEM()` rather than manually dereferencing `gobj + 0x2C`, aligning the code with project conventions and reducing brittle raw pointer arithmetic.
Modeled the item variable region as a typed union member instead of repeatedly casting from raw item offsets.

Preserved exact item-relative layout by adding leading padding from `0xDD4` to `0xDF4`; this was the important matching detail because the union begins at `item+0xDD4`, not at the first known Arwing laser field.
Used offset-expression padding sizes such as `0xDF4 - 0xDD4` and `0xE18 - 0xDF8`, making the intended layout easier to audit than magic byte counts alone.
Verified the issue with raw byte comparison after objdiff fallback display masked the mismatch.
decomp_lessons: When adding an item-var struct under `Item::xDD4_itemVar`, struct field comments should be item-relative, but the struct layout starts at `item+0xDD4`; include padding for any gap before the first real field.
A field intended to represent `item+0xE38` inside `xDD4_itemVar` must sit at struct offset `0xE38 - 0xDD4`, not at offset `0xE38` from the start of the struct.
Objdiff display can obscure byte-level differences in fallback modes; confirm suspicious matches with raw byte comparison.
Raw pointer arithmetic cleanups are safest when paired with an accurate struct/union layout, not just replaced syntactically.
PR descriptions for decomp work should accurately list only committed functions; comments noted that functions mentioned in the description were not actually part of the committed change.
naming_conventions: Item variable structs follow the pattern `it<Name>_ItemVars`, here `itArwingLaser_ItemVars`.
The corresponding `Item::xDD4_itemVar` union member used lowercase item naming: `arwinglaser`.
Unknown fields retained offset-style names such as `xDF4`, `xE18`, `xE38`, `xE40`, and `xE44`.
Padding fields were named `_pad0`, `_pad1`, `xE32_pad`, `xE34_pad`, and `xE3C_pad`, with comments documenting item offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `*(Item**) ((u8*) gobj + 0x2C)` with `GET_ITEM(gobj)` when the access is the standard item payload lookup.
Replace raw item offset reads with typed union access only after the union member struct has correct leading padding.
For item-var structs inside `xDD4_itemVar`, calculate padding relative to the union base offset `0xDD4`.
Use raw byte comparison when objdiff appears to show identical instructions but matching still fails or a hidden mismatch is suspected.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Pointed out that functions mentioned by the author were not committed in the PR.

review_feedback.author: IsaacGemal
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the PR name/description were misleading and apologized while learning project conventions.

follow_up_queries: Find other item code still using `*(Item**) ((u8*) gobj + 0x2C)` instead of `GET_ITEM(gobj)`.
Audit other `xDD4_itemVar` structs for missing leading padding between `item+0xDD4` and the first known field.
Search for raw reads from `item + 0xE38` that could be converted to typed item-var access.
Check whether `it_802E8418` and `it_802E8784` documentation in nearby PR descriptions accurately reflects committed changes.
